channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Michigan offense goes dormant after starting fast, springs back to life late to put away Huskies
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Michigan looked as if it would run away from Washington early in the College Football Playoff championship game Monday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's next for Jim Harbaugh? Michigan coach faces decision on whether to stay or go back to NFL
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Michigan's climb back to the summit of college football ended with its 34-13 win over Washington in the College Football Playoff championship game on Monday night. Next, the Wolverines and their fans wait and see if Jim Harbaugh stays or goes.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington star Michael Penix Jr. takes pounding, disappointing loss in college finale
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Michael Penix Jr. couldn’t complete the final chapter in his storybook season Monday night. The quarterback with two surgically repaired knees threw for 255 yards with a touchdown and two interceptions, taking a pounding as No.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIE RIEKEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CMU MSOC star Goalie Diego Chavez to return next seaon
item.description: 2023's RMAC Goalie of the Year is back to try and once again chase a championship. As a team captain, he'll undeniably provide a massive boost to his squad next fall.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Jim Harbaugh delivers a national title. Corum scores 2 TDs, Michigan overpowers Washington 34-13
item.description: Jim Harbaugh and No. 1 Michigan completed a three-year climb to a national championship by beating No. 2 Washington 34-13 Monday night in the College Football Playoff title game.
item.dc:creator: Ralph D. Russo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michigan football finishes the job, wins first national title in 26 years
item.description: Michigan football defeated Washington on Monday night to win its first national championship in 26 years.
item.dc:creator: Derick Hutchinson, Ken Haddad, Local 4

item.title: Coby White scores 27 as Bulls hold on to beat Hornets 119-112 in OT
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Coby White scored 27 points in his return to his home state, Andre Drummond added 21 points and 15 rebounds and the Chicago Bulls overcame a 39-point performance from Terry Rozier to beat the Charlotte Hornets 119-112 in overtime on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ja Morant's season is over because of a shoulder injury. The Grizzlies say he needs surgery
item.description: Ja Morant's season is over, with the Memphis Grizzlies announcing Monday night that their star guard needs surgery to repair a tear in his right shoulder. Morant got hurt in practice on Saturday, the Grizzlies said.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Grizzlies' Ja Morant out for the season with shoulder injury following suspension return
item.description: The Memphis Grizzlies announced on Monday night that Ja Morant will miss the remainder of the 2023-24 season due to a shoulder injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan's Donovan Edwards starts National Championship with 2 touchdowns in 1st quarter
item.description: The Michigan Wolverines got off to a fast start on Monday night as running back Donovan Edwards scored on a 41-yard run against Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 49ers assistant GM Peters reportedly targeted by other NFL teams for promotion
item.description: Peters, who has interviewed for GM jobs before, is just the latest 49ers front-office face to be coveted by opposing teams.
item.dc:creator: Michael Nowels, The Mercury News

item.title: Sharks update: What’s missing from William Eklund’s game right now?
item.description: San Jose Sharks forward William Eklund is on an extended goal drought going into Tuesday's game with the Toronto Maple Leafs
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: With top pick in the draft and big questions about franchise's future, Bears are on the clock
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — The Chicago Bears are on the clock. And all eyes are on general manager Ryan Poles. The Bears enter the offseason with the No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jaguars fire defensive coordinator Mike Caldwell, seven assistants after late-season collapse
item.description: Defensive coordinator Mike Caldwell and seven of his assistants ended up taking the fall for the biggest late-season collapse in Jacksonville Jaguars history.
item.dc:creator: Mark Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iconic Brazilian soccer player, coach Mario Zagallo dies at 92
item.description: He was the first person to win World Cup championships as a player and as a manager. He also was the last living member of Brazil's starting team from 1958, the country's first championship squad.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: For Saints coach Dennis Allen, players' decision to score late vs. Atlanta was no mutiny
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Saints coach Dennis Allen is taking a nuanced view of his players' decision to disobey coaches' orders and hand the ball off to Jamaal Williams for a 1-yard touchdown run in the final minute of a season-ending 48-17 victory over Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Virginia lawmakers set to take up proposal to help fund new arena for Capitals, Wizards
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — Advocates of Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin's plan to build a new professional sports arena and entertainment district in northern Virginia say the project would be a generational job-creator so lucrative it will pay for itself.
item.dc:creator: SARAH RANKIN and MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bucs with Bowles: Coach previews playoff rematch against Eagles on MNF
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers won their third consecutive NFC South title on Sunday with a 9-0 win over the Carolina Panthers.
item.dc:creator: Dan Lucas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rams' Puka Nacua sets rookie records for yards and catches
item.description: Puka Nacua couldn't have scripted a much better ending to his first regular season in the NFL than what played out on Sunday. A touchdown catch. Two NFL records. A celebration with mom. And a win for the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW AP Pro Football Writer, KTLA 5

item.title: Justin Fields faces offseason of uncertainty as Bears debate whether to take a QB with No. 1 overall pick
item.description: GREEN BAY, Wis. (AP) — Chicago Bears quarterback Justin Fields has been through all this before. That helps explain why he refuses to spend the offseason dwelling on his uncertain future as the Bears debate whether to keep him as their starter or select his replacement with the No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Falcons fire coach Arthur Smith hours after completing 3rd straight 7-10 season
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Arthur Smith, who inherited a rebuilding project in his first NFL head coaching job and failed to lift Atlanta from its playoff drought, was fired late Sunday night, hours after completing his third straight losing season with the Falcons.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors fans exit early, boo team during blowout loss to Raptors
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- It has been tough times recently for the Golden State Warriors as they've fallen two games below .500 after another home loss at Chase Center on Sunday night when they lost handily against the Toronto Raptors (15-21).
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: This Day in Bulldog History: January 7th
item.description: FRESNO, Calif. (KSEE) - On January 7th, 2006, the Fresno State men's basketball team hosted Utah State in the Bulldogs' WAC opener. Fresno State was 7-4 overall, while Utah State was 9-3.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Marden, KSEE, CBS

item.title: Bulldog Insider preview: Kalen DeBoer, Washington prep for CFP national championship game
item.description: HOUSTON (KSEE) - There is one game left in the college football season: the CFP National Championship. On Monday night in Houston, Texas, Washington plays Michigan. Most of Washington's coaching staff was in Fresno as recently as 2021. "You want to empower your staff.
item.dc:creator: Angelique Martinez, KSEE, CBS

item.title: 'Playoffs are always the best': Bucs fans celebrate NFC South win
item.description: The Bucs are traveling back to Tampa after securing the win on the road against the Carolina Panthers on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Bucs were favored to win 1 game this season – now they're heading to the playoffs
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) -- It appears as though the Tampa Bay Buccaneers got the last laugh. Following former Buccaneer and star quarterback Tom Brady's retirement, the national media lashed out at the Bucs, favoring the team to win only a singular game during the 2023-24 regular season.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alex Smith elected to College Football Hall of Fame
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Quarterback Alex Smith has been elected to the College Football Hall Fame, becoming the first Utah player ever to receive that honor. Legendary Utes coach Ike Armstrong was elected in 1957.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah State shuts down #13 Colordado State, 77-72
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It was a special night at The Spectrum, and the Utah State fans felt like celebrating. Great Osobor scored 20 points and had 14 rebounds and Mason Falslev added 18 points to lift the Aggies over No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: BYU drops Big 12 opener to Cincinnati, 71-60
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - In its much-anticipated Big 12 Conference opener, the BYU basketball team turned in its worst offensive performance of the season, and fell at home to Cincinnati, 71-60. BYU had been averaging 90 points per game, and winning by an average of 28 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Draymond Green reinstated from suspension by NBA after missing 12 games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Draymond Green was reinstated by the NBA from his suspension on Saturday after the Golden State star missed 12 games for hitting Phoenix center Jusuf Nurkic in the face on Dec. 12.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Draymond Green reinstated by NBA after suspension
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- After being handed an indefinite suspension last month, Draymond Green has been reinstated by the NBA, the league announced Saturday afternoon. Green's suspension ended up being 12 games.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Carlsbad hoops makes San Diego history at Holiday Classic
item.description: Carlsbad High School head basketball coach Clark Allard and his squad are riding high after becoming the fifth San Diego area team to win the national division title at the 33rd Torrey Pines Holiday Classic.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors G Chris Paul breaks hand, needs surgery
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors will be without Chris Paul for the foreseeable future. The veteran guard suffered a fractured left hand in the Warriors' win over the Detroit Pistons on Friday and will need surgery, the team confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Olympic sprinter Oscar Pistorius freed after serving nearly 9 years in prison for killing girlfriend
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Olympic sprinter Oscar Pistorius, a double-amputee who became a global star competing at his sport's highest level while running on carbon-fiber blades, was released from prison on Friday after serving nearly nine years for killing his girlfriend, the model Reeva Steenk
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, NQOBILE NTSHANGASE and MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 4 Rams, 2 Chargers named to 2024 NFL Pro Bowl
item.description: The 2024 NFL Pro Bowl rosters were announced Wednesday and L.A.'s two teams are both represented. The Los Angeles Rams will have four representatives in the 2024 Pro Bowl games, including a future first ballot hall-of-famer and two newcomers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Athlete of the Week: Memorial Tigers Bradley Manso
item.description: EAST RUTHERFORD, N.J. (PIX11) -- From 113 players representing every team in the New Jersey Super Conference to eight finalists to one young man of the year.
item.dc:creator: Perry Sook, PIX 11

item.title: Worthy to NFL, Norris Jr. to showcase
item.description: Tuesday's Central Valley sports news included a Central High alum declaring for the NFL Draft, and an outgoing Fresno State defensive star from Sanger getting an opportunity to impress scouts at a college all-star showcase.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bemis, KSEE, CBS
